name: MRI Lumbar Spine — Likely Denial
description: Patient with low back pain, only 2 weeks conservative treatment, no neurological findings.
expected_outcome: UHC: ~65% denial, Aetna: ~80% denial, Cigna: ~60% denial
text:
CLINICAL NOTE

Patient: Maria Garcia
DOB: 07/22/1978
Date of Service: 01/20/2026
Provider: Dr. Robert Chen, DO — Primary Care
NPI: 9876543210
Member ID: AETNA-44567123

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Low back pain for 2 weeks.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Garcia is a 47-year-old female presenting with low back pain that started 2 weeks ago. She describes the pain as a dull ache, rated 5/10, located in the central lower back. No radiation to the legs. No numbness or tingling. Pain is aggravated by prolonged sitting. She works as an office administrator.

CONSERVATIVE TREATMENT HISTORY:
- OTC ibuprofen for 2 weeks — some relief
- No physical therapy attempted
- No prior imaging

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
- Lumbar ROM: Full range of motion with mild discomfort at end-range flexion
- Straight leg raise: Negative bilaterally
- Motor: 5/5 throughout bilateral lower extremities
- Sensory: Intact to light touch bilaterally
- Reflexes: 2+ and symmetric bilaterally
- Gait: Normal

ASSESSMENT:
1. Low back pain, unspecified (M54.5)

PLAN:
Requesting MRI lumbar spine without contrast (CPT 72148) to evaluate for disc pathology.

Signed: Dr. Robert Chen, DO
Date: 01/20/2026
